data.title: Fitch Warns of Inflation Risks for Armenia and Georgia Due to Long-Term Shocks
data.description: Fitch Ratings has highlighted potential inflation risks and widening external deficits for Armenia and Georgia, particularly as these countries are net energy importers. According to Jin10, the agency noted that long-term economic shocks could exacerbate these issues, impacting the financial stability of both nations. The report emphasizes the vulnerability of economies reliant on energy imports, suggesting that sustained disruptions could lead to increased inflationary pressures and fiscal challenges.

data.title: XKONG Unveils Decentralized Health Network Strategy in Hong Kong
data.description: Hong Kong-based AI health management platform XKONG has announced its 'Decentralized Health Network' strategy during the 'AI × RWA: The Action Economy' event. According to ChainCatcher, the company plans to deploy over 3,500 health service terminals in Hong Kong, creating a health ecosystem comprising AI diagnostic tools, the XKONG app, and AI health assistant KoKo. User interactions and health activities will be converted into token rights for accessing AI services, establishing a closed loop from 'user behavior' to 'AI consumption.' XKONG aims to expand this model to over 10 countries and regions by 2026, using Hong Kong as a prototype.

data.title: DefiLlama founder: After Arbitrum seized the hacker's funds, Aave's bad debts on that L2 platform will be reduced by at least 80%.
data.description: According to Odaily Planet Daily, DefiLlama founder 0xngmi posted on the X platform that, after verifying the data, if Arbitrum prioritizes using the seized funds for the AAVE market on Arbitrum, Aave will have no bad debts on Arbitrum, assuming all rsETH share the impairment. Furthermore, by only guaranteeing the value of the mainnet rsETH (ignoring the value of the mapped rsETH on Layer 2), Aave will reduce its bad debts on Arbitrum by 80%, from $88 million to $17 million.

data.title: U.S. and Iran Face Challenges in Pursuing Lasting Peace Agreement
data.description: As the ceasefire between the United States and Iran nears its conclusion, both nations are confronted with significant challenges that must be addressed to secure a lasting peace agreement. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the complexities involved in the negotiations. Key issues include mutual trust, regional security concerns, and the potential impact on global diplomatic relations. The resolution of these matters is crucial for establishing a stable and enduring peace between the two countries.

data.title: Galaxy Digital Reduces ETH Short Positions Amid Market Adjustments
data.description:
Galaxy Digital has been actively reducing its ETH short positions over the past 16 hours, according to BlockBeats. The associated address (0xcac) has decreased its holdings by 905 ETH, valued at approximately $2.09 million, while still maintaining an unrealized profit of $2.6 million and a total position size of $7.4 million.

Since March, this address has significantly reduced its positions, selling off BTC and ETH spot holdings and closing short positions. The total spot sales have reached $11 million, completing a full liquidation, while the short position size has decreased from $35 million to the current $13.8 million. During this period, the funding rate settlement has yielded approximately $3.5 million in profits.

The address has reportedly received multiple on-chain funds from Galaxy Digital and has been executing a neutral arbitrage strategy on Hyperliquid. This strategy typically involves selling spot assets and closing short positions or buying spot assets and increasing short positions to maintain a risk-neutral stance.

data.title: Turkey's Central Bank Initiates Gold-for-Lira Swap Auction
data.description: Turkey's central bank has commenced a week-long gold-for-lira swap auction, offering a total of 4 tons of gold. According to Jin10, this traditional method aims to manage liquidity and stabilize the local currency. The auction reflects ongoing efforts by the central bank to bolster the Turkish lira amid economic challenges. The initiative is part of broader measures to address currency volatility and support the country's financial stability.

data.title: 12 European banks jointly develop MiCA compliant euro stablecoin
data.description: A consortium of 12 European banks, led by Qivalis, has selected Fireblocks to provide infrastructure for the development of a euro-denominated stablecoin compliant with the MiCA regulatory framework. The stablecoin, planned for launch in the second half of 2026 and subject to approval from the Dutch Central Bank, will be backed 1:1 by euro reserves and issued electronically under Dutch regulation. It will primarily target institutional settlement, fund management, and asset tokenization. Fireblocks will provide tokenization, wallet, and compliance tools, including identity verification and sanctions screening capabilities. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Euro-Denominated Stablecoin Set for 2026 Launch by Qivalis Consortium
data.description: Fireblocks is managing the issuance and distribution of a euro-denominated stablecoin, backed by 12 European banks within the Qivalis consortium. According to NS3.AI, the token is slated for release in the latter half of 2026 and will be regulated by the Dutch Central Bank under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCAR). Euro-pegged assets currently account for $650 million of the $305 billion stablecoin market.

data.title: LayerZero-Based Apps Predominantly Use Minimal Verifier Network Setup
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Wu Blockchain has highlighted data from Dune indicating that 47% of applications built on LayerZero utilize a 1-of-1 Decentralized Verifier Network configuration. This setup is the same minimum configuration employed by KelpDAO. Furthermore, only 5% of OApps have adopted more robust settings that necessitate multiple validators, suggesting a preference for simpler verification processes among developers.

data.title: Whale Closes BTC Short Position with Minor Loss
data.description:
A major cryptocurrency holder, identified by the address starting with 0x616, has closed a Bitcoin short position valued at $4.06 million, according to ChainCatcher. The position was closed at an average price of $76,218, resulting in a minor loss of approximately $2,614.

Following the closure, the same address initiated a new short position above the previous closing price, at $76,670, involving 52.6 BTC, valued at around $4.03 million, indicating an attempt to reestablish the position.

data.title: Iranian media: No delegation has yet departed for Pakistan to participate in negotiations with the US.
data.description: According to Iranian state media reports, no Iranian delegation has yet departed for Pakistan to participate in peace talks with the United States.

data.title: Market Cautious on U.S.-Iran Peace Agreement Prospects
data.description:
A recent analysis indicates that an account with a success rate exceeding 75% has invested over $480,000 in the position that a permanent peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran will not be reached by April 22. According to Odaily, the opening price for this position was 83.1¢, with the current price at 82.5¢, resulting in an unrealized loss of approximately $3,750.

The U.S. and Iran have been engaged in a second round of negotiations facilitated by Pakistan. However, factors such as port blockades and regional tensions contribute to the high uncertainty of reaching a comprehensive agreement in the short term. The market remains generally cautious in its expectations.

data.title: The Iranian delegation's preliminary working team has reportedly arrived in Islamabad.
data.description: It was learned on April 21 local time that sources in Pakistan's diplomatic circles stated that the preliminary working team of the Iranian delegation had arrived in Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan. Iranian officials have not yet confirmed this. (CCTV)

data.title: An account with a win rate exceeding 75% purchased $480,000 worth of goods; the statement reads, "A permanent peace agreement between the US and Iran will not be reached before April 22."
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that accounts with a win rate exceeding 75% have purchased over $480,000 in positions that would not result in a permanent peace agreement between the US and Iran by April 22nd, with an opening price of 83.1¢ and a current price of 82.5¢, resulting in a floating loss of approximately $3,750. Recently, the US and Iran have advanced to the second round of negotiations under Pakistan's mediation, but due to factors such as port blockades and regional tensions, the uncertainty of reaching a comprehensive agreement in the short term remains high, and market expectations are generally cautious. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing in prices.

data.title: AI TRENDS | a16z Crypto Highlights Identity as Key Challenge for AI Agents
data.description: a16z crypto has identified identity as the primary challenge for AI agents, rather than intelligence. According to NS3.AI, the firm noted that AI agents already outnumber human employees by approximately 100 to 1 in the financial services sector. To address this, a16z crypto proposed a 'Know Your Agent' model, suggesting that blockchains could facilitate portable identities, programmable wallets, and verifiable attestations across various platforms.

data.title: Crypto Holders Targeted in Violent Home Invasion in France
data.description: A couple in Le Chesnay-Rocquencourt, near Paris, fell victim to a home invasion where attackers impersonating police officers allegedly coerced them into transferring nearly $1 million in Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, French authorities are currently investigating the incident as an armed robbery and organized criminal conspiracy. This case highlights a growing trend of 'wrench attacks,' where criminals use physical force to target cryptocurrency holders instead of relying on digital hacking methods.

data.title: Binance Cuts Portfolio Margin Collateral Ratios for WBTC, CAKE, ETHFI, INJ, TRUMP Effective April 24
data.description: According to a Binance Announcement, Binance will reduce collateral ratios for five assets under Portfolio Margin effective April 24 at 06:00 UTC, with WBTC cut from 70% to 60%, CAKE and ETHFI each from 60% to 50%, INJ from 60% to 40%, and TRUMP from 50% to 30%. Under PM Pro, TAO's tiered collateral structure will also be revised. Separately, Binance Futures will lower leverage and margin tiers for 19 USDⓈ-M perpetual contracts beginning 06:30 UTC the same day, including ARKMUSDT, BIGTIMEUSDT, BOMEUSDT, and others. Existing positions will be affected; users with active futures grid strategies are advised to adjust before the changes take effect.

data.title: Cobo AI Enhances Agent Control with Structured Permission System
data.description:
On April 21, Cobo AI's Growth Lead, Brad Bao, discussed advancements in agent control at an event titled 'Decoding Web 4.0: When AI Agents Take Over On-Chain Permissions.' According to BlockBeats, Cobo is restructuring the logic of agent fund control through a structured permission system.

In Cobo's products, the relationship between users and agents is defined by an executable 'Policy/Agreement,' rather than a simple wallet authorization. This agreement includes four core elements: Intent, Path, Rules, and Termination. This approach transforms the traditional 'all-or-nothing' permission control into a negotiable, auditable, and revocable fine-grained governance mechanism.

Cobo employs Multi-Party Computation (MPC) technology at the execution level to ensure that even if an agent behaves abnormally, the system is attacked, or prompt injection risks occur, users maintain control over their funds. No single entity can bypass constraints to independently transfer assets. Brad Bao emphasized that within this framework, agents have sufficient freedom to complete complex tasks, but their actions are always confined within verifiable boundaries.

He stated that this system essentially builds a layer of 'trust and insurance infrastructure' in the agent economy. By combining 'contractual permissions and secure computation,' it allows for controlled delegation of agent actions, enhancing automation efficiency while ensuring user asset security and outcome certainty.

data.title: European Stock Markets Open with Declines
data.description: European stock markets opened with widespread declines. According to ChainCatcher, the German DAX index fell by 1.15%, the UK's FTSE 100 index decreased by 0.55%, France's CAC 40 index dropped by 1.12%, the Euro Stoxx 50 index declined by 1.24%, and Italy's FTSE MIB index saw a decrease of 1.36%.

data.title: Bithumb launches Story (IP) flexible staking product
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bithumb is launching the Story (IP) flexible staking product, following its existing 15 assets including ETH, SOL, and TRX. The incentive program will run from April 21st to 27th, with a total prize pool of 200 million Korean Won. IP is the native token of the Story protocol. Story is positioned as an on-chain IP infrastructure for AI, supporting IP registration, programmable licensing, and automated revenue distribution for datasets, models, and AI-generated content.
